meta_title: For Students: freelance pitching to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Freelance Pitching to Escape Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Break out of tutorial hell by mastering freelance pitching. Learn to build real-world SaaS apps using Antigravity and Claude Code as a student developer.
content.answer_block: You are stuck in 'tutorial hell' because you lack the pressure of a real client. Freelance pitching forces you to solve actual business problems, not just follow videos. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative development, you can move from student hobbyist to professional SaaS builder.
content.intro:
The screen glows with another finished 'to-do list' app, but the silence from your portfolio is deafening. You've mastered the syntax, yet you feel like an impostor, trapped in a cycle of endless tutorials that never quite translate into a career. The gap between following a guide and shipping a production-ready application feels like an unbridgeable chasm.

But the chasm isn't a lack of talent; it's a lack of stakes. Real growth happens the moment you have to explain a technical decision to a non-technical client. By shifting your focus from 'learning to code' to 'coding to solve,' you transform your development environment into a engine for business value, using modern AI tools to accelerate your path to your first paid contract.

steps.title: Identify a Micro-SaaS Niche
steps.description: Find a local business with a manual process (e.g., scheduling, inventory). Don't build for 'everyone'; build for one specific person.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze a local dry cleaner business model. Identify three manual spreadsheet-based tasks that can be automated into a simple SaaS dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'research industry pain points for dry cleaners'

steps.title: Architect the MVP Scope
steps.description: Define the minimum viable feature set. Scope creep is the enemy of the student developer.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a feature roadmap for a dry cleaner scheduling app. Prioritize user authentication and automated email reminders as the core MVP.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a database schema for an appointment booking system'

steps.title: Bootstrap with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate your boilerplate code, ensuring a professional foundation that follows industry best practices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a full-stack Next.js application with Supabase integration. Include boilerplate for user login, dashboard layout, and database connectivity.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize project structure using the scaffolded output'

steps.title: Iterative Development with Claude Code
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write your business logic. Focus on unit tests to ensure your app doesn't break as you scale.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust function for handling appointment cancellations, including edge cases for database transaction rollbacks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add --file src/lib/scheduling.ts 'implement cancellation logic with error handling'

steps.title: The Pitch Presentation
steps.description: Prepare a demo that focuses on the ROI for the client, not the tech stack.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a 5-minute pitch script for a dry cleaner owner. Focus on how the app saves them 10 hours of manual work per week.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a presentation outline based on the app features'

pro_tips: Never lead with your tech stack; clients care about time saved and revenue generated, not your choice of framework.
Use Claude Code to generate end-to-end tests before writing feature code to ensure your MVP remains stable.
Always offer a 'maintenance retainer' post-launch to turn a one-time project into a recurring income stream.
Document your build process in a public repository to act as a living portfolio for future employers.
If a client asks for a feature that takes more than 2 days to build, suggest a manual workaround or a simplified alternative.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is functional, set up a simple landing page using your project and start reaching out to three prospects in your local area to demo the tool.
content.conversion_block: Stop building for the void and start building for the market. Your transition from student to professional developer begins with your first real-world deployment. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
